Weekly Docs Report

docs.wal.app Analytics

2026-04-27 to 2026-05-03 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260504T160324Z

Summary Traffic Top Pages Referrals Top Referrer URLs Page↔Theme Takeaways Topics User Engagement Sprint
Overview

Executive Summary

Kapa Questions
562
Themes Identified
9
Visitors (7d)
13,804
Stable daily traffic averaging ~1,972 visitors/day with a modest peak on Apr 30 (2,234).
Pageviews (7d)
21,771
Views per visit is low at 1.4, indicating most users read one or two pages and leave.
Bounce Rate (7d)
81%
Consistently high across all periods (81–82%), suggesting most visitors do not explore beyond their landing page.
Avg Visit Duration (7d)
38 seconds
Very short sessions across all time windows (37–38s), typical of documentation sites where users quickly scan for specific answers.
Views per Visit (7d)
1.4
Low engagement depth; the 14-day and 30-day figures are identical (1.39–1.40), showing no trend improvement.
Visitors (30d)
57,884
The 7-day period represents ~24% of 30-day traffic, consistent with a roughly flat weekly cadence.
Blob storage operations and Walrus Sites deployment dominate developer questions, while docs.wal.app traffic is concentrated on the homepage and Sites components page with an 81% bounce rate indicating most visitors arrive with a specific question. Spam and off-topic queries consume nearly 19% of chatbot capacity, and pricing confusion combined with network endpoint errors represent the most actionable documentation gaps this sprint.
Plausible

Traffic Overview

docs.wal.app had 13,804 visitors and 21,771 pageviews over the 7-day period (Apr 27–May 3, 2026), with a high bounce rate of 81% and a short average visit duration of 38 seconds. Traffic is heavily dominated by direct/none sources (~96% of visitors). The homepage and the Sites Components page are overwhelmingly the top destinations, together accounting for over half of all pageviews. Daily traffic is relatively stable, ranging from ~1,827 to ~2,234 visitors/day with a mild peak on Apr 30–May 1.

Stable daily traffic with a mid-week peak

Daily visitors ranged narrowly from 1,827 (Apr 28) to 2,234 (Apr 30), with pageviews peaking at 3,501 on May 1. The pattern shows a consistent weekday/weekend dip on Apr 28 (Mon) and May 2 (Sat), typical of developer-focused documentation.

Persistently high bounce rate across all time windows

Bounce rate holds at 81–82% across 7d, 14d, and 30d, indicating this is a structural characteristic of the audience—users arrive with a specific question, find or don't find it, and leave. No improvement trend is visible.

GitHub referrals drive highest-quality sessions

GitHub visitors average 557s on site vs. 37s for direct traffic and 90s for Google. Although only 12 visitors, this cohort is the most engaged, suggesting developer-facing content resonates strongly with repo visitors.

Duplicate URL issue for /walrus-sites/intro

Both /walrus-sites/intro and /walrus-sites/intro.html received exactly 515 visitors and 680 pageviews, indicating a URL duplication issue that splits SEO equity and may cause confusion for crawlers.

Sites and operator content drives secondary engagement

Beyond the homepage, the most-visited pages are Sites-focused (components, site builder, portals) and operator-guide content, suggesting two primary user personas: site builders and node operators. These segments warrant dedicated content investment.

Plausible

Top 20 Pages

# Page Insight
1 / By far the most visited page with 8,635 visitors and 11,608 pageviews—over 53% of all pageviews. Low bounce (9%) and 46s avg time suggest it serves as a meaningful entry hub.
2 /docs/sites/introduction/components Second most visited with 3,813 visitors and 5,290 pageviews. High scroll depth (100%) and 21s time indicate users quickly scan component reference content.
3 /docs/getting-started Third with 1,462 visitors and 1,744 pageviews. Solid 30s time-on-page and 14% bounce; a key onboarding entry point.
4 /walrus-sites/intro 515 visitors and 680 pageviews with 100% scroll depth. Likely a legacy or redirected URL alongside /walrus-sites/intro.html which has identical metrics.
5 /walrus-sites/intro.html Duplicate of /walrus-sites/intro with identical visitor/pageview counts (515/680), suggesting two URL variants serve the same content without consolidation.
6 /docs/operator-guide 113 visitors, 135 pageviews, 6% bounce and 51s time—operators who land here are engaged and explore further.
7 /docs/legal/privacy 105 visitors, 131 pageviews. Routine legal page traffic, low bounce (10%) suggests it's linked from within the site.
8 /docs/operator-guide/storage-nodes/commission-governance 93 visitors, 121 pageviews with a very high 88% bounce rate and 78s time—users find this specific reference page directly and leave after reading.
9 /docs/examples/checkpoint-data 87 visitors, 99 pageviews, 11% bounce and 57s time—one of the better-engaged example pages.
10 /docs/getting-started/advanced-setup 54 visitors, 78 pageviews. Low bounce (13%) and 73s time suggest developers are thoroughly reading advanced setup instructions.
11 /docs/sites/getting-started/installing-the-site-builder 53 visitors, 68 pageviews, 13% bounce and 48s time—steady interest in site builder installation.
12 /docs/legal/walrus_general_tos 55 visitors, 61 pageviews. Low bounce (9%) and 36s time; likely visited as part of onboarding or compliance checks.
13 /docs/system-overview/available-networks 28 visitors but 58 pageviews with a 103% 'bounce rate' anomaly (likely a data artifact). Low time (46s) suggests quick reference lookups.
14 /docs/sites 31 visitors, 53 pageviews, very low bounce (7%) and 78s time—users landing here navigate deeper into sites documentation.
15 /docs/system-overview 34 visitors, 46 pageviews, 13% bounce and 66s time—a well-engaged overview section entry point.
16 /docs/http-api/storing-blobs 22 visitors, 37 pageviews, 16% bounce and 85s time—developers integrating the HTTP API spend meaningful time here.
17 /search 34 visitors, 37 pageviews with 16% bounce and 96s time—users actively searching spend the longest time on this page, indicating search is a key navigation tool.
18 /docs/system-overview/core-concepts 28 visitors, 36 pageviews, 22% bounce and 62s time—moderate engagement with foundational concepts content.
19 /docs/operator-guide/storage-nodes 26 visitors, 31 pageviews, very low bounce (7%) and 63s time—operators navigating the node setup flow.
20 /docs/walrus-client 18 visitors, 28 pageviews with a high 62% bounce rate and 75s time—users arrive directly but spend time reading before leaving.
Plausible

Referral Sources

Source Type Insight
Direct / None Organic Dominates with 13,278 visitors (~96% of all traffic), 82% bounce rate and 37s duration. The overwhelming direct share suggests strong bookmark/direct URL usage or significant dark social/AI referral traffic not captured by referrer headers.
docs.wal.app Organic 351 visitors from internal self-referrals, 49% bounce and 63s duration—users navigating between docs pages with referrer set to the same domain.
Google Organic 155 visitors with the best engagement profile: 61% bounce rate and 90s duration—organic search visitors are the most engaged audience segment.
wal.app Organic 15 visitors referred from the main Walrus app, 60% bounce and 21s duration—brief cross-property visits.
GitHub Organic 12 visitors from GitHub (specifically MystenLabs/walrus repo) with only 50% bounce and an outstanding 557s (9.3 min) avg duration—the highest quality traffic source by engagement.
walrus.xyz Organic 6 visitors, 67% bounce and just 2s duration—very low-quality referral, users likely bounce immediately.
thewalrussessions.wal.app Organic 3 visitors, 100% bounce—likely an event or community site sending occasional traffic that doesn't engage with docs.
Bing Organic 2 visitors but 864s (14+ min) avg duration—tiny sample but extraordinarily high engagement; Bing searchers may be deeply researching.
hackergpt.app AI / Chatbot 2 visitors, 100% bounce—an AI-powered hacker/GPT tool referring minimal traffic with no engagement.
DuckDuckGo Organic 2 visitors, 100% bounce—negligible privacy-focused search traffic this week.
walrus.site Organic 2 visitors, 100% bounce—minimal referrals from a Walrus-hosted site.
events Organic 2 visitors, 100% bounce—likely a tagged campaign or event-based referral with no follow-through engagement.
AI Referral Detail

hackergpt.app

2 visitors with 100% bounce; AI-powered tool sending minimal low-engagement traffic to docs.

Cross-Signal

Page ↔ Kapa Theme Correlations

High-traffic pages mapped to the Kapa themes they correspond to.

Page Related Kapa Theme Insight
/docs/sites/introduction/components Walrus Sites & Site Builder High scroll depth with 5,290 pageviews aligns with repeated bot questions about deployment and object IDs.
/docs/getting-started Blob Storage Operations 1,744 pageviews at 14% bounce mirrors the bot's most-repeated 'How do I store data?' question.
/docs/http-api/storing-blobs Blob Storage Operations 85s dwell time signals developers integrating the HTTP API need more complete code examples.
/docs/operator-guide/storage-nodes/commission-governance WAL Token & Staking 88% bounce with 78s dwell suggests users find staking governance content but leave with unanswered questions.
/docs/system-overview/available-networks Network Configuration & Endpoints High pageview-to-visitor ratio suggests repeated lookups for endpoint and package ID references.
/docs/examples/checkpoint-data SDK & TypeScript Integration 57s dwell and low bounce indicate developers actively reading examples but SDK code is thin.
/docs/sites/getting-started/installing-the-site-builder Walrus Sites & Site Builder Steady 48s dwell aligns with bot uncertainty on site-builder installation steps.
/docs/walrus-client Blob Storage Operations 62% bounce despite 75s dwell suggests the walrus-client reference page leaves key questions unanswered.
Analysis

Notable Takeaways

Spam consumes nearly one-fifth of all chatbot capacity

105 out of 562 questions classified as Off-Topic & Spam

Bot abuse is significantly inflating uncertain-answer counts and distorting theme-level confidence metrics.

The single most-asked question is verbatim-repeated at scale

Blob Storage Operations theme has 155 questions, the highest of any theme by 66%

Identical phrasing repeated dozens of times suggests users are not finding the answer on their first visit.

Network endpoint and package ID errors drive high uncertainty in a critical path

Network Configuration & Endpoints: 25 uncertain out of 50 total questions (50% uncertainty rate)

Outdated or incorrect endpoint references are blocking integration work and eroding developer trust.

GitHub referrals produce sessions 15x longer than direct traffic

GitHub referral avg duration 557s vs 37s for Direct/None traffic

Developers arriving from the MystenLabs/walrus repo are the highest-intent audience and warrant better cross-linking.

Kapa

Developer Topics

Across all chunks, blob storage operations and how to store/retrieve data dominate user questions, followed by Walrus Sites deployment, storage costs, and network configuration. A large volume of off-topic and spam interactions also consume significant bot capacity. Questions grouped by docs.sui.io category, then by specific topic.

Q: How can someone upload a video ti walrus that can encrypt blob ID

Q: 为什么blob的sender和owner都不是我
A: 从你给的信息和现有文档来看，只能推断出**几种常见原因**，但没法精确看到你那笔具体交易，所以下面是基于文档的可能性说明，而不是对你那条对象的链上“诊断”。 先说明一点： Walrus 里每次 `walrus store` 都会在 Sui 上创建一个 `blob::Blob` 对象，这个对象有一个 **owner**（拥有者地址），而交易本身也有一个 **sender**（发起交易的地址）。…

Sprint Planning

Recommended Actions

Doc Actions
high Documentation Partial

Add a 'Store Your First Blob' Primary Quickstart

Create a new top-level quickstart page covering CLI, HTTP API, and TypeScript SDK with copy-paste examples.

Blob storage is the most-asked theme with 65 uncertain answers, and Getting Started has 1,744 pageviews showing onboarding demand.

↑ Reduces repeat identical questions and lowers the 42% uncertainty rate on blob storage operations.
Docs cover CLI storing blobs (https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/storing-blobs.md) and HTTP API (https://docs.wal.app/docs/http-api/storing-blobs.md) and TypeScript SDK (https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/sdks.md) separately, but there is no single unified 'Store Your First Blob' quickstart page combining all three with copy-paste examples.
high Documentation Partial

Publish a Single Always-Current Endpoint and Package ID Reference

Consolidate all mainnet and testnet aggregator, publisher, and package IDs onto one auto-updated reference page.

Network configuration has a 50% uncertainty rate across 50 questions; the available-networks page shows anomalous repeat lookups.

↑ Eliminates the most common integration-blocking errors caused by stale or incorrect endpoint references.
The public aggregators and publishers page exists (https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/public-aggregators-and-publishers.md) and available networks page exists, but there is no single consolidated, auto-updated reference page with all mainnet/testnet aggregator, publisher, and package IDs in one place.
high Documentation Partial

Add Walrus Sites Deployment and Object ID Persistence FAQ

Extend the Sites components and site-builder pages with a FAQ covering deploy vs update, object ID persistence, and framework compatibility.

Sites & Site Builder has 28 uncertain answers and the components page is the second-highest traffic page with 5,290 pageviews.

↑ Reduces deployment confusion and prevents developers from accidentally creating duplicate site objects.
The sites troubleshooting page (https://docs.wal.app/docs/sites/troubleshooting.md) and site-builder usage page exist, but there is no dedicated FAQ covering deploy vs update distinction, object ID persistence across redeployments, and framework compatibility.
high Documentation Partial

Publish a Storage Pricing Explainer with Worked Examples

Create a dedicated pricing page with WAL/SUI split examples, epoch cost tables, and a prominent link to the cost calculator.

Storage Costs has a near-50% uncertainty rate across 55 questions and egress fee questions go unanswered.

↑ Reduces purchase hesitation and clarifies the dual-token cost model for developers at the evaluation stage.
A Storage Costs page exists (https://docs.wal.app/docs/system-overview/storage-costs.md) with WAL/SUI split and per-epoch pricing info, but it lacks worked numeric examples, epoch cost tables, and a prominent link to a cost calculator.
Tooling Actions
high Tooling Partial

Improve walrus CLI Error Messages for Missing Config and PATH Issues

Add actionable error messages to the walrus binary when client_config.yaml is missing or the binary is not in PATH.

Repeated bot questions show developers hitting 'could not find a valid Walrus configuration file' and 'walrus: command not found' with no clear remediation.

↑ Reduces first-run failure rate and decreases support burden from installation and configuration errors.
The troubleshooting and network errors pages (https://docs.wal.app/docs/troubleshooting/network-errors.md) cover some config errors, but the recommendation is about improving the walrus binary's own error messages, which is a code-level change not addressed in documentation.
medium Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Add TypeScript SDK Validation and Descriptive Errors for Common Misconfigurations

Improve the @mysten/walrus SDK to surface clear errors when Upload Relay DNS fails, WASM is missing, or blob object ownership is unexpected.

SDK questions show 14 uncertain answers and browser errors like ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED and writeBlob buffer failures with no SDK-level guidance.

↑ Reduces integration time for TypeScript developers and eliminates silent failures during blob upload flows.
No documentation exists addressing TypeScript SDK validation errors for Upload Relay DNS failures, missing WASM, or unexpected blob object ownership; the SDK page (https://docs.wal.app/docs/typescript-sdk/sdks.md) provides no such guidance.
DX Actions
medium Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Build a Walrus CLI Blob Explorer Command

Add a new walrus blob-status command that returns certification status, expiry epoch, owner address, and current availability.

Developers repeatedly ask how to verify upload success, check blob certification, and map blob IDs to object IDs with no single inspection tool.

↑ Gives developers a first-class debugging surface and reduces post-upload support questions about certification and ownership.
No 'walrus blob-status' command is documented; the reading-blobs page (https://docs.wal.app/docs/walrus-client/reading-blobs.md) mentions status queries but does not describe a dedicated blob-status subcommand returning certification status, expiry epoch, owner address, and availability.
docs.wal.app Docs Report · 2026-04-27 to 2026-05-03 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260504T160324Z
